paragraphs.body: I got my first bottle freshman year of high school. Aromatic, woody, fresh, warm, spicy, fruity, herbal, citrusy, and more. I picked it because it was versatile and could be worn anywhere. Soon after, I got a new one. Spicy, vanilla, tobacco, powdery, and sweet. This was a brisk winter fragrance I wanted for that specific time of the year when it’s cold. Soon another: green, aquatic, floral, fresh, musky, and woody. This was for the summer, but these don’t usually last as long. I kept going, getting more and more bottles of cologne to add to a shelf that went from being in my closet to now a stand alone shelf in my room that I proudly call my fragrance collection.

paragraphs.body: Fragrances have personalities just like people. Everybody can be summed up into a little 100-milimiter bottle. Some have underlying accords that you can only smell after they dry down on skin. Spicebomb Extreme by Victor & Rolf opens up from the atomizer with harsh notes of pepper and tobacco, but as it dries down has pleasant notes of vanilla and warm spice. One of my closest friends is a Spicebomb. We used to sternly compete and go at each other’s throats over who was better at baseball, but once high school came around, we became fond of each other and have been close ever since.

annotations.text: Fragrances have personalities just like people.
annotations.comment: I'm interested though I don't quite know what you mean? Also, without the context and summary of the "existing conversation" that leads into this essay, your reader is a bit adrift.

paragraphs.body: Some bottles have the opposite effect. Drying with harsh notes, they usually don’t last long, and I’m not friends with these people anymore. I don’t normally think about these bottles. They’ve been pushed back on my shelf while others remain in the front. Some bottles are duds, and with one spray, an instant look of bitter disdain crosses the face. After studying a plethora or aromas, I started to realize what lasts and what doesn’t. For me, this bottle, specifically Versace Blue Jeans, is also an old friend from freshman year of high school who I had a brusque falling out with. The scent is known to be outdated in the fragrance community. He sits in the back left corner of my shelf, collecting dust like a bottle of fine wine aging in a cellar. I check on him occasionally but never pick him up and spray him again. Even though the scent isn’t for me, it totally works for someone else. That’s the beauty of fragrances. He has his own friends and supporters, just like other people who adore that specific scent. The bottle Is a part of my permanent collection though just as that friend will forever be a part of my life.

annotations.text: Versace Blue Jeans, is also an old friend from freshman year of high school who I had a brusque falling out with. The scent is known to be outdated in the fragrance community.

paragraphs.body: Certain scents have the power to turn heads when wearing them. Valentino Born in Roma Coral Fantasy is a loud fragrance that hits with a juicy and vibrant red apple sweetness, energizing and almost triggering a smile. Valentino is my longest childhood friend. Every time he is in the room, everybody knows. This fragrance comes out on special occasions, the same way I see my friend only when we are back from college and our breaks line up or for the holidays.

annotations.text: Valentino Born in Roma Coral Fantasy is a loud fragrance that hits with a juicy and vibrant red apple sweetness, energizing and almost triggering a smile. Valentino is my longest childhood friend. Every time he is in the room, everybody knows.

paragraphs.body: Sitting at the very top and front of my shelf with fingerprints all over the glistening bottles with wear and tear on the caps are those in my daily rotation. Versatile, they can be taken anywhere, seen a lot, and always dependable. This is my group of closest friends. We may not go to the same school anymore, but we still talk everyday.

Finally, there are niche fragrances whose longevity, complexity, and uniqueness makes them so appealing. Fragrances like these are harder to come by because they are expensive, made in limited supply, or both. I originally had one that I saved up a long time to buy, but I have a few more now. I will use these fragrances sparingly, take them throughout the rest of life with me, and keep them in pristine shape with their own personal stand on my shelf towering above the other fragrances front and center. These select fragrances represent my family, the most important figures in my life who will always be with me. Every person has a specific spot on my shelf, and every scent holds a special value that is unique to everybody.

annotations.text: Finally, there are niche fragrances whose longevity, complexity, and uniqueness makes them so appealing.
annotations.comment: This sentence might reveal some values of importance to you. Do you value complexity and uniqueness? In what ways, specifically?

paragraphs.body: Potential quotes to incorporate: “Perfumes have a special way of taking us through time and places, evoking emotions, and making a lasting impression on others.”

“Perfumes are a bit of magic in our everyday lives. They are transparent liquids capable of evoking memories, lifting up spirits, attracting or repulsing, boosting confidence, and leaving an impression—all with one spray to the skin.”
“A personally chosen scent can bring a bit of magic to your life.”
